[{"title": "Ag and Food Statistics: Charting the Essentials - Food Prices and Spending | Economic Research Service", "url": "https://www.ers.usda.gov/data-products/ag-and-food-statistics-charting-the-essentials/food-prices-and-spending", "snippet": "March 10, 2026 -In 2024, U.S. consumers, businesses, and government entities spent $2.58 trillion on food and beverages. ... Average annual food-at-home prices were 1.2 percent higher in 2024 than in 2023. For context, the 20-year historical level of retail food price inflation (2004\u201323) is 2.7 percent per year."}, {"title": "From Grocery Carts to Doorsteps: 2024 Food Spending | Market Intel | American Farm Bureau Federation", "url": "https://www.fb.org/market-intel/from-grocery-carts-to-doorsteps-2024-food-spending", "snippet": "August 22, 2025 -From 1997 to 2024, U.S. spending on food at home rose from $363 billion to $1.091 trillion, while food away from home surged from $336 billion to $1.539 trillion."}, {"title": "How much do Americans spend on food every year? | World Economic Forum", "url": "https://www.weforum.org/stories/2021/07/cost-of-food-america-united-states-health-environment/", "snippet": "July 26, 2021 -Such costs include human health complications, biodiversity loss, enviornmental impact and the effects of the economy. Collectively, they add an estimated $2.1 trillion, bringing the true cost of food to$3.2 trillion annually."}, {"title": "Americans spend $1 trillion on food each year. The true cost is three times higher | The Independent", "url": "https://www.independent.co.uk/climate-change/news/food-cost-health-environment-america-b1885723.html", "snippet": "July 17, 2021 -Unaccounted livelihood costs \u2013 including \u201cunlivable\u201d wages, lack of benefits, including childcare and health insurance, and occupational hazards among food workers \u2013 add roughly $100bn to the true cost of food. The report examined 14 key metrics to measure those costs \u2013 including impacts to health, the environment, biodiversity, working conditions and livelihoods. But across many of the areas, \u201ccommunities of colour bear a disproportionate burden\u201d of such impacts, according to the report. According to the report, rates of diabetes are 1.7 times higher among Latino communities and 1.5 times higher among Black Americans compared to white Americans."}, {"title": "2024 Consumer Dining Trends: How Americans Are Spending on Restaurants and Takeout - Escoffier", "url": "https://www.escoffier.edu/blog/world-food-drink/consumer-dining-trend-statistics/", "snippet": "September 25, 2024 -Based on a review of USDA data, ... the US Department of Agriculture\u2019s Economic Research Service (ERS),in 2023, Americans spent $1.5 trillion on FAFH; they spent $1.1 trillion on FAH during the same year...."}]
